questions.title: Abdominal Assessment
questions.text: Identify the correct area on the abdomen where you would auscultate bowel sounds first.
questions.rationale: When auscultating bowel sounds, the nurse should start in the right lower quadrant over the ileocecal valve area, where bowel sounds are typically the most active. The right lower quadrant (RLQ) contains the cecum and appendix, which are areas with high intestinal activity. Starting at this location allows the nurse to immediately identify if bowel sounds are present without having to move the stethoscope multiple times.

questions.title: Prioritizing Nursing Actions
questions.text: Place the following nursing actions in the correct order when responding to a patient experiencing an anaphylactic reaction to a medication.
questions.rationale: When responding to an anaphylactic reaction, airway management is always the priority, followed by breathing support and circulation. Assessing the patient's airway, breathing, and circulation (ABCs) is the first step. Since anaphylaxis can cause rapid airway compromise, administering epinephrine quickly is crucial to prevent further deterioration. After these life-saving measures, establishing IV access allows for medication administration, followed by monitoring vital signs to assess the patient's response to interventions. Documentation and notification are important but come after stabilizing the patient.

items.text: Assess patient's airway, breathing, and circulation

questions.title: Patient Lab Results Interpretation
questions.text: Review the following laboratory results for a 72-year-old male patient admitted with complaints of fatigue, shortness of breath, and pallor.
questions.rationale: This patient's lab values indicate iron deficiency anemia, as evidenced by low hemoglobin, low hematocrit, low MCV (microcytic), low serum iron, elevated TIBC, low ferritin, and elevated RDW. The combination of these findings is consistent with iron deficiency, which in an elderly male patient warrants further investigation for possible GI bleeding, which is the most common cause of iron deficiency anemia in this demographic.

questions.text: Based on the laboratory results, which type of anemia does this patient most likely have?
Which assessment finding would you expect to see in this patient? Select all that apply.
